1.solution:
With 5 laborers (75% efficiency): production = 20×10×0.75×30×10 = 45000 m/month.
With 4 laborers (70% efficiency): production = 20×10×0.70×30×10 = 42000 m/month.
Lost revenue = 3000×$4 = $12000. Saved salary = $11000.
Net = −$12000+$11000 = −$1000 (loss).
Answer: B (1000 loss).

2.solution:
f fill pipes (rate 1/8 each) + d drain pipes (rate 1/6 each), f+d=12.
f/8 − d/6 = 1/3 → 3f/24 − 4d/24 = 8/24 → 3f−4d=8.
3f − 4(12−f) = 8 → 7f=56 → f=8.
Answer: 8.

3.solution:
Total work = 33×30 = 990 man-days. Day k has (45−k) men.
Work after n days = Σ(k=1 to n)(45−k) = n(89−n)/2.
After 43 days: 43×46/2=989. After 44 days: 44×45/2=990.
On day 44: 1 man remaining, finishes last 1 man-day exactly.
Answer: C (44).

5.solution:
Arak rate=1/36, Manasa rate=1/30 (1/3 work in 10 days → full in 30 days).
Sheetal=50% of Manasa=1/60. Per 2-day cycle: (1/36+1/30)+(1/36+1/30+1/60)=11/180+14/180=25/180=5/36.
After 7 cycles (14 days): 35/36. Remaining=1/36.
Day 15 (A+M only): 1/36÷(11/180)=5/11≈0.4545 days. Total=14+5/11≈14.45.
Answer: B (14.45).

6.solution:
A full rate=1/20, B full rate=1/30. Together full=1/12. Last 5 min fills 5/12.
Partial rates: A×4/5=1/25, B×5/6=1/36. Combined partial=(1/25+1/36)=61/900.
61t/900+5/12=1 → 61t/900=7/12 → t=7×75/61=525/61≈8.61 min.
Nearest integer = 9 minutes.
Answer: 9.

10.solution:
Alok=45 days, Brijesh=40 days. They collaborate t days, then Alok alone 56 days at half efficiency.
Work: t/45+t/40+56/(2×45)=1 → t(1/45+1/40)+28/45=1 → t×17/360=17/45 → t=8.
Answer: A (8).
